title: "podman inspect --type image --format exists <image>" reports a not-friendly error when image doesn't exist in local storage
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?**:

[//]: # Uncomment only one, leave it on its own line:

# **kind bug**

[//]: # **kind feature**

**Description**
When we run ```podman inspect --type image --format exists <image>``` against a non-existing image, we end up with a long, not really nice message like:
```
2018-11-19 21:42:01 |         "b'error getting image \"docker.io/tripleomaster/centos-binary-cinder-volume:3ed8ac0e93367a02ad53d9fa93467057724b6621_fd8eb74b\": unable to find \\'docker.io/tripleomaster/centos-binary-cinder-volume:3ed8ac0e93367a02ad53d9fa93467057724b6621_fd8eb74b\\' in local storage\\n'",
```
It should return something like ```the image <image> does not exist``` instead, and at least not as an error, especially since we are testing its existence.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. run the command against an image not yet present in the local storage

2.

3.

**Describe the results you received:**
Long, not nice, not userfriendly message, with an "error" flag

**Describe the results you expected:**
A small, informative message stating the image doesn't exist, without "error" in it

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:       0.11.1.1
Go Version:    go1.10.2
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.5-dev
  Conmon:
    package: podman-0.11.1.1-3.git594495d.el7.x86_64
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 1.12.0-dev, commit: ccde1bf8e093607ecf92b404368033f645cad755-dirty'
  Distribution:
    distribution: '"centos"'
    version: "7"
  MemFree: 874045440
  MemTotal: 8365137920
  OCIRuntime:
    package: runc-1.0.0-55.dev.git2abd837.el7.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.0'
  SwapFree: 8557686784
  SwapTotal: 8588881920
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 8
  hostname: undercloud.localdomain
  kernel: 3.10.0-862.14.4.el7.x86_64
  os: linux
  rootless: false
  uptime: 59m 56.33s
insecure registries:
  registries:
  - 192.168.24.1:8787
  - 192.168.24.3:8787
registries:
  registries:
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - quay.io
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - registry.centos.org
store:
  ContainerStore:
    number: 1
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions:
  - overlay.override_kernel_check=true
  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
  ImageStore:
    number: 19
  RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage

```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: @cjeanner the error you show looks like python?
@baude it's the output of the command formatted in python indeed

So, without the python formatting, i.e. running directly from a shell:
```
sudo podman inspect --type image --format exists asasas
error getting image "asasas": unable to find 'asasas' in local storage: unable to find a name and tag match for asasas in repotags
```
I'd expect a shorter message, like ```image asasas does not exist``` - without "error" nor the rest, especially with the ```--format exists``` option. Even a single "no" would be enough, with that format option.
im probably in the minority here, but im ok with the error; lets see who is weighs in

The issue is, when we have issues during the deploy, most of the time ppl search for "error" and other "failed" terms, and when they see that, they are "Ah, that's the issue", while it isn't in fact. This raises false flags, and we lose time searching in the wrong direction :/.

Let's see how it goes then ;).

@cjeanner So you think this is not an error?  Or you want to check if the image exists?, Why not just eat the error and check the status 

```
[dwalsh@localhost libpod]$ sudo podman inspect --type image --format exists asasas 2> /dev/null
[sudo] password for dwalsh: 
[dwalsh@localhost libpod]$ echo $?
125
```

@rhatdan because if a real error happens, we need to catch it.
I think that we'll parse the output in our Python code and ignore this string in particular, so we can still catch other errors. I'll test that and close that bug when it's done.

I've pushed https://review.openstack.org/619313 to make our inspect command quiet. I'll close this issue when I don't see that message any more with my patch.

Thanks all for your help.

How about adding `--quiet flag` (or `--exit-code` to follow git) that would make  podman quiet by default unless another kind of error appears.

When doing "exists" it is expected not to find an image, so no output should be given unless there is a reason that makes it impossible for podman to return a trustable exit code.

I think I would prefer to add a new command to 
```
podman images exist foobar
```
Which would return 0 or 1.
And return errors if it was anything other then ENOENT.
Id be happy to do that.  I often wished for similar with containers.  I assume @rhatdan you'd be cool with both? if so, ill make it happen.
@baude +1 with the idea! we'll probably use it.
FYI I updated https://review.openstack.org/#/c/619313/ to be prepared to use ```podman images exist```.
are you guys OK with a 0 exit code being successful and a non-zero meaning it didn't exist?

Yes +1

On Sun, Nov 25, 2018, 3:51 PM Brent Baude <notifications@github.com wrote:

> are you guys OK with a 0 exit code being successful and a non-zero meaning
> it didn't exist?
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you were mentioned.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/1845#issuecomment-441472094>,
> or mute the thread
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/ABnCoZn2N_wG3aWp4kida9moGtAtzTeeks5uywK2gaJpZM4YtFJ0>
> .
>

Do you want us to eat the error message as we talked about earlier?  Here I show the error message but it is something we could easily not output.
``` 2018-11-25 14:56:55 ⌚  localhost in ~/git/libpod
± |exists S:1 U:2 ?:22 ✗| → sudo podman image exists a
unable to find 'a' in local storage: unable to find a name and tag match for a in repotags

 2018-11-25 14:57:05 ⌚  localhost in ~/git/libpod
± |exists S:1 U:2 ?:22 ✗| → echo $?
125

 2018-11-25 14:57:06 ⌚  localhost in ~/git/libpod
± |exists S:1 U:2 ?:22 ✗| → sudo podman image exists demoweb

 2018-11-25 14:57:11 ⌚  localhost in ~/git/libpod
± |exists S:1 U:2 ?:22 ✗| → echo $?
0


```

like so 

``` 2018-11-25 14:59:05 ⌚  localhost in ~/git/libpod
± |exists S:1 U:2 ?:22 ✗| → sudo podman image exists a

 2018-11-25 14:59:09 ⌚  localhost in ~/git/libpod
± |exists S:1 U:2 ?:22 ✗| → echo $?
1
```

A message is good. In some cases we'll hide it, in others we'll print it.

---
Emilien Macchi

On Sun, Nov 25, 2018, 3:59 PM Brent Baude <notifications@github.com wrote:

> like so
>
> ± |exists S:1 U:2 ?:22 ✗| → sudo podman image exists a
>
>  2018-11-25 14:59:09 ⌚  localhost in ~/git/libpod
> ± |exists S:1 U:2 ?:22 ✗| → echo $?
> 1
>

Are we good to close now with the new command upstream?
Is there already a released package with the new output? If so, I think we're all good :+1:
